title: Delhi Liquor Policy Case LIVE Updates: CBI Moves HC Against Kejriwal's Discharge; Rekha Gupta Says 'Evidence Tampered'
content:
Delhi Excise Policy Case News Live: Delhi’s Rouse Avenue Court on Friday discharged former Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal, former Deputy Chief Minister Manish Sisodia and all other accused in the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) case linked to the alleged Delhi Excise Policy scam.
Kejriwal hailed the decision as “historic”, accusing Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah of hatching a “conspiracy” to defame him and the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP). He said the court’s order confirmed that there was not an iota of evidence against him.
Meanwhile, the CBI has approached the High Court, challenging the Rouse Avenue Court’s order discharging former Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal, former Deputy Chief Minister Manish Sisodia and all other accused in the alleged Excise Policy Scam case.
The court had earlier observed that the agency failed to present material sufficient to proceed against them. The order was passed by Special Judge Jitender Singh, who refused to frame charges in the corruption case, discharging all 23 accused persons.
‘IMPLICATED WITHOUT MATERIAL’
The court noted that no prima facie case was made out against the accused and observed significant shortcomings in the prosecution’s case.
While pronouncing the judgment, Arvind Kejriwal had been implicated without any cogent material linking him to the alleged offence.
The court held that it did not find any criminal conspiracy in the case and that the CBI’s allegations “lacked substance”.
Follow for live updates
Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta, reacting to legal relief to Arvind Kejriwal, said everyone knew the evidence in the liquor policy case had been tampered with. “This is the lower court’s decision…We all know how evidence (in liquor policy case) was tampered with,” she told reporters.
“If you (Arvind Kejriwal) were right, then why did you withdraw the liquor policy as soon as the investigation started?…The courts had also expressed their worry on this case and traces of money laundering was found. Even today you are doing ‘nautanki’ (drama),” she added.
Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Aaditya Thackeray commended Arvind Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia after they were discharged in the Delhi excise policy case and accused the Centre of defaming opposition leaders before elections. “We are very proud of Arvind Kejriwal and the case he has fought along with Manish Sisodia… The hope is that the BJP will apologise to Arvind Kejriwal, and the independence of the institution now remains,” he said.
Arvind Kejriwal slammed the Congress for its reaction to his discharge in the excise policy case, “Kejriwal went to jail. Did Robert Vadra go to jail? Sanjay Singh went to jail. Did Rahul Gandhi go to jail? Manish Sisodia went to jail. Did Sonia Gandhi ji went to jail? What is Congress saying? Does it have no shame?”
The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has moved the Delhi High Court, challenging the Rouse Avenue Court’s order discharging former Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal and others in the liquor excise policy case, CBI sources told CNN-News18.
Reacting to Arvind Kejriwal’s discharge, BJP MP Manoj Tiwari said, “This is a decision of a lower court. The CBI is going to the higher court. All the evidence against the corrupt is solid, and we are confident that the criminals involved in the scams will lose.”
BJP national spokesperson RP Singh said there are other corruption cases against AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal, which will go for trial. “As per the CAC report, there was scam in building roads, schools and hospitals, all these cases will be under trial,” he said.
Arvind Kejriwal challenged the BJP and PM Modi, saying if elections are held again in Delhi and the BJP wins over 10 seats, he will leave politics. “The people of Delhi are tired of your failures,” he said.
Arvind Kejriwal also said his mother was very sick and had to be admitted to a hospital while he was imprisoned in the excise policy case. He also said Manish Sisodia’s wife suffered from multiple sclerosis.
In a strong message to PM Modi, Kejriwal said, “You filed so many cases against us, you sent ED and CBI after us, you even sent us to jail, even then you could do nothing to Kejriwal. Now PM Modi has only one option, to kill Kejriwal. You have tried everything, now you would have to kill me.”
Arvind Kejriwal in his press conference also said he was repeatedly questioned why he disappeared from the public view after his bail, saying he preferred to keep quiet till his name was cleared of all charges.
“I am an honest man, it affects me when someone calls me dishonest. It affects me when I am sent to jail and my family faces taunts. I had decided that I will keep quiet until I am proven innocent in front of the nation.”
Arvind Kejriwal said he had always led an honest life and never earned a single penny through dubious means, while accusing the BJP of defaming him. He said the court’s decision proved that he was “kattar imaandar”.
AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal demanded an apology from Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah, alleging that they hatched the “most disgusting conspiracy in independent India” to finish the Aam Aadmi Party.
In response to the acquittal of AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal, BJP MP Manan Kumar Mishra said, ”In crying and doing drama, Arvind Kejriwal is very good at it. What is the answer to this?”
AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal said the Delhi court, in its 600-page order, said there is not the slightest evidence to even have a case on the liquor excise policy issue. He said the verdict of the court was “historic”, which exposed a conspiracy against him and the Aam Aadmi Party.
Arvind Kejriwal held a press conference on Friday, where he thanked the lawyers for his discharge in the Delhi excise policy case. He also attacked the BJP and central agencies, accusing them of ‘conspiring’ to finish the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) through a false case.
RJD leader Tejashwi Yadav claimed that central agencies were filing cases against opposition leaders and accused the BJP of defaming people. “The court has also said that a case should be filed against the CBI’s IO (investigating officer) as well. Agencies keep filing cases against opposition parties in the wrong manner. The BJP uses this to defame people. We too have faith in the judiciary that in the coming times, we will also get justice,” he told reporters.
Congress spokesperson Shama Mohamed questioned how the evidence against Arvind Kejriwal in the Delhi liquor policy case had disappeared. “Let’s understand certain things. No one has said that one is innocent. They’re saying there is no evidence… There was evidence before; suddenly, the evidence has disappeared,” she said.
Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) leader Atishi on Friday said that Arvind Kejriwal’s honesty is before entire nation.
“Arvind Kejriwal’s ‘kattar imaandaari’ is before the entire nation today… On one side, it is the BJP’s conspiracy, hunger for power and misuse of central agencies; on the other side is Arvind Kejriwal’s and AAP’s honesty,” she said.

title: CBI Moves Delhi High Court Against Relief To Kejriwal, Sisodia In Excise Policy Case
content:
The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has approached the Delhi High Court, challenging the Rouse Avenue Court order clearing former Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal and former Deputy Chief Minister Manish Sisodia in the Delhi Excise Policy case, according to CBI sources.
The move comes soon after the special court granted relief to the two senior Aam Aadmi Party leaders, holding that the allegations against them did not stand up to judicial scrutiny.
While pronouncing the order, the court observed that the alleged central conspiratorial role attributed to them by the investigating agency could not be substantiated.
The court noted that the accusations placed before it “failed judicial scrutiny."
In its observations, the court found no criminal intent on the part of Manish Sisodia and stated that the prosecution’s conspiracy theory “cannot survive against one constitutional authority."
The court concluded that the material presented was insufficient to proceed against the two leaders at this stage.
CBI’S CASE AND ALLEGATIONS
The CBI had registered the case in connection with alleged irregularities in the now-scrapped Delhi excise policy.
The agency filed its first chargesheet in 2022 and later submitted multiple supplementary chargesheets as part of the investigation.
According to the agency, a “south lobby" allegedly paid Rs 100 crore to influence the excise policy in its favour.
In total, 23 accused were charge-sheeted in the matter, including Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia, K Kavitha, Kuldeep Singh, Narender Singh, Vijay Nair, Abhishek Boinpally, Arun Ramchandra Pillai, Mootha Goutam, Sameer Mahendru, Amandeep Singh Dhall, Arjun Pandey, Butchibabu Gornatla, Rakesh Joshi, Damodar Prasad Sharma, Prince Kumar, Chanpreet Singh Rayat, Arvind Kumar Singh, Durgesh Pathak, Amit Arora, Vinod Chauhan, Ashish Mathur and P Sarath Chadra Reddy.
During arguments before the trial court, the CBI maintained that the alleged offence of criminal conspiracy must be examined in its entirety and argued that the sufficiency of evidence should be assessed during trial rather than at the discharge stage.
Represented by Additional Solicitor General DP Singh and advocate Manu Mishra, the agency contended that adequate material existed to frame charges against all accused persons.
DEFENCE ARGUMENTS BEFORE COURT
Senior advocate N Hariharan, appearing for Kejriwal, argued that there was no incriminating material linking his client to the alleged conspiracy.
The defence submitted that the fourth supplementary chargesheet naming Kejriwal merely reiterated earlier allegations and that he was performing his official duties as Chief Minister.
Hariharan further pointed out that Kejriwal was not named in the original chargesheet or in the first three supplementary chargesheets and was included only in the fourth.
The defence also questioned the basis of further investigation and the evidentiary value of certain statements, including that of approver Raghav Magunta.

title: 'Unmitigated Disaster': Jyotiraditya Scindia Takes Swipe At Congress For AI Summit Protest
content:
Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia on Thursday strongly criticised the Indian Youth Congress (IYC) for staging a shirtless protest during the AI Impact Summit in New Delhi, terming the disruption “an unmitigated disaster" at a global platform meant to showcase India’s technological leadership. Speaking at the News18 Rising Bharat Summit 2026, Scindia said the summit represented India’s national interest and was not a party event.
Members of the Indian Youth Congress gathered outside the high-security venue and staged a dramatic shirtless protest, raising slogans and attempting to march toward the site where global leaders and delegates had assembled. With over two dozen heads of state and international representatives in attendance, security was heightened. Delhi Police detained and later arrested several protesters for breaching barricades and attempting to disrupt the proceedings.
Reacting to the incident, Scindia said, “It was an unmitigated disaster. The AI Impact Summit was not a BJP programme. It was Bharat’s programme. You had 26 heads of state visiting India because everyone realises India is the next growth potential in the world." He argued that protests of this nature at an international forum damaged the country’s image at a time when India is emerging as a major technology and innovation hub.
In a sharp political swipe, he added, “If you want to exhibit the vacuousness of desh bhakti, then good luck to you. India has rejected you multiple times. It is the people in that arena there that took these people down," asserting that electoral mandates had already delivered their verdict.
On Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, Scindia was brief, saying, “I have nothing to say to Rahul Gandhi." However, responding to Gandhi’s criticism of the India-US trade deal, he said, “If you are so anti your own country in your own mind, then I don’t think this country has any place for you."
Scindia positioned the controversy as a clash between India’s global ambitions and what he described as disruptive political theatrics.
The News18 Rising Bharat Summit 2026 is a two-day national platform hosted in New Delhi that brings together political leaders, policymakers, industry captains, innovators, thinkers and cultural voices to spotlight India’s expanding role on the global stage and chart the country’s future trajectory across technology, policy, trade, culture and social development.

title: 'Truth Has Won': Arvind Kejriwal Breaks Down After Delhi Court Clears Him In Liquor Policy Case
content:
Arvind Kejriwal turned emotional and broke down before cameras on Friday after a Delhi court discharged him and former Deputy Chief Minister Manish Sisodia in the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) case linked to the now-scrapped Delhi Excise Policy, calling the verdict a victory of truth and justice.
Speaking to reporters after the order, an emotional Kejriwal said the verdict reaffirmed his long-held claim of innocence.
“I am not corrupt. The court has said that Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia are honest," the former Chief Minister said.
“BJP was making allegations against us, but the court acquitted everyone, truth has prevailed," Kejriwal said, breaking down during the interaction.
“We always said that the truth emerges victorious. We have full faith in the Indian legal system. (Union Home Minister) Amit Shah and (Prime Minister) Modi ji together hatched the biggest political conspiracy to finish the Aam Aadmi Party, and five big leaders of the party were put in jail."
“The sitting Chief Minister was dragged out of his house and put in jail. Kejriwal is not corrupt. I have only earned honesty in my life," he added.
https://images.news18.com/ibnlive/uploads/2026/02/tweeload_din1v3jh-2026-02-68eab43061680bb06648899428108cab.mp4
Former Deputy Chief Minister Manish Sisodia also reacted to the verdict, saying the outcome strengthened faith in constitutional institutions.
“We are proud of our Constitution," Sisodia said, crediting the judicial process for ensuring justice after a prolonged legal battle.
Meanwhile, Arvind Kejriwal’s wife, Sunita Kejriwal, took to X and wrote, “In this world, no matter how powerful one becomes, one cannot rise above Shiva Shakti. Truth always prevails."
Separately, speaking to reporters, she said, “We faced a lot of problems, but God was with us. We are grateful to those who stood with us during such difficult times."
The order was passed by Special Judge Jitender Singh of the Rouse Avenue Court, who discharged all 23 accused in the matter and declined to frame charges in the corruption case.
The court held that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case and flagged serious deficiencies in the investigation.
The court also rapped the CBI for implicating the accused without sufficient material and noted that the voluminous chargesheet contained several lacunae unsupported by witness statements or evidence.
COURT FLAGS INVESTIGATIVE LACUNAE
The court’s order underscored the importance of a fair investigation for ensuring a fair trial.
The judge criticised the investigative process, pointing to gaps in evidence and inconsistencies in the prosecution’s case.
With the refusal to frame charges, all accused persons in the matter stand discharged.
During hearings, the CBI had argued that the alleged conspiracy should be viewed in its entirety and that the sufficiency of evidence ought to be tested during trial.
The defence, however, maintained that there was no incriminating material linking Kejriwal to the alleged wrongdoing and questioned why his name appeared only in a later supplementary chargesheet.
THE DELHI EXCISE POLICY CASE
The case stems from allegations surrounding the formulation and implementation of Delhi’s excise policy, which was probed by the CBI.
The agency had alleged that a “south lobby" paid around Rs 100 crore to influence the policy in its favour.
The court had reserved its order on February 12 after hearing detailed arguments from both the prosecution and the accused, including Kejriwal and Sisodia, before pronouncing its decision on February 27.

title: Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia Cleared By Delhi Court In Liquor Policy Case
content:
Delhi’s Rouse Avenue Court on Friday discharged former Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal, former Deputy Chief Minister Manish Sisodia and all other accused in the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) case linked to the alleged Delhi Excise Policy scam, observing that the agency failed to present material sufficient to proceed against them.
The order was passed by Special Judge Jitender Singh, who refused to frame charges in the corruption case and discharged all 23 accused persons.
COURT FINDS NO EVIDENCE AGAINST ACCUSED
The court held that no prima facie case was made out against the accused and noted significant shortcomings in the prosecution’s case.
The judge observed that Arvind Kejriwal had been implicated without any cogent material connecting him to the alleged offence.
The court emphasised that a fair investigation is fundamental to ensuring a fair trial and found that the evidence placed on record did not substantiate the allegations levelled by the CBI.
The judge also said the voluminous chargesheet filed by the agency contained several lacunae and was not supported by witness statements or corroborative material.
As reported by Live Law, the judge held that the CBI failed to make out even a prima facie case against Sisodia, leading to his discharge from the matter along with the other accused.
The order came after the Rouse Avenue Court had reserved its decision on February 12, following detailed arguments from both the CBI and the accused.
The case relates to alleged irregularities in the now-scrapped Delhi Excise Policy.
The CBI had alleged that a “south lobby" paid around Rs 100 crore to influence the policy in its favour.
The agency filed its first chargesheet in 2022 and later submitted multiple supplementary chargesheets naming 23 accused, including Kejriwal and Sisodia.
During arguments, the CBI maintained that the offence of criminal conspiracy should be viewed in its entirety and argued that the adequacy of evidence should be tested during trial proceedings.
The defence, however, contended that there was no incriminating material linking the accused to the alleged conspiracy.

title: Rising Bharat Summit 2026 Highlights: PM Modi Slams TMC, Says Ayushman Bharat Scheme Not Implemented In Bengal
content:
News18 Rising Bharat Summit 2026 Day 1 Highlights: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the News18 Rising Bharat Summit 2026, which brought together top national leadership and international delegates at Bharat Mandapam on February 27, Friday, reflecting the confidence and capabilities driving India’s rise.
During the speech, PM Modi hailed the nation’s internal strength and said that the country has realised its potential in the last 11 years.
Built around the theme — “Strength Within” — the two-day summit brings together leaders, policymakers, and industry voices to shape a future powered by India’s own resolve and ambition. This is where national purpose meets global ambition, transforming resilience into a roadmap for excellence on the world stage.
Among the other dignitaries were: Union Ministers Piyush Goyal, Ashwini Vaishnaw,  and Jyotiraditya Scindia, among others.
Addressing the gathering, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “Ayushman Bharat scheme has not been implemented in Bengal till today.”
He added, “If the intention had been sincere, this scheme providing free treatment up to ₹5 lakh for the poor would not have been stopped in Bengal.”
Referring to housing initiatives, he said, “Under the PM Awas Yojana, permanent houses are being built for the poor across the country.”
Citing Tamil Nadu, PM Modi said, “In Tamil Nadu, 9.5 lakh permanent houses have been allotted to poor families, but construction of 3 lakh houses has stalled because the DMK government is not taking interest in building homes for the poor.”
He concluded, “Because the intention is not sincere.”
Addressing the role of the opposition in a democracy, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “In democracy, the role of the opposition is not just about blind opposition but about presenting an alternative vision. That is why the enlightened public of the country is teaching a lesson to Congress.”
Addressing the gathering, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “A nation’s progress is determined not by today’s comforts, but by preparation for tomorrow. The hard work done with foresight is the foundation of a self-reliant, strong, and prosperous India by 2047. And no matter how much the Congress may protest, we will continue working relentlessly.”
Continuing his criticism, PM Modi said, “There is anger in the country over this act of Congress. That is why, to justify their wrongdoing, they brought Mahatma Gandhi forward. The Congress party does the same thing whenever it wants to hide its mistakes — it brings Bapu to the forefront — and when it wants to claim credit, it gives all the recognition to one family.”
He also said, “In the name of ideology, Congress has now been reduced to merely a ‘toolkit’ of opposition. This mindset of blind opposition has grown so much that it does not miss any opportunity to belittle the country on any stage or platform.”
Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched a sharp attack on Congress over its protest during the AI Summit, calling it an attempt to undermine a moment of national pride. He said, “The AI Summit was a moment of pride for the entire country, but the country’s oldest party tried to turn the national celebration into a spectacle. In front of foreign guests, Congress not only exposed itself but also revealed its ideological bankruptcy.”
He further added, “When frustration and disappointment from failure fill the mind and arrogance takes over, such thinking that defames the country comes to the surface.”
Addressing the Rising Bharat Summit 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi criticised Congress over its shirtless protest during the AI Summit. He said, “incidents like this occur due to a mindset of arrogance, which is portrayed by Congress,” linking the episode to what he described as a larger pattern of behaviour.
Speaking at the Rising Bharat event, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “there was a time when india was just a consumer of a technology, but today india is manufacturing it.” He further added, “There was a time when India was importer of new technologies, now we are exporter of new technology.”
Highlighting India’s infrastructure growth, PM Modi said, “world is amazed with our digital infrastructure.” He also noted, “India has world third largest metro network,” pointing to rapid urban and connectivity expansion.
Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “When the hidden strength within a nation awakens, it achieves new accomplishments.” He underlined the importance of national confidence and collective resolve in driving progress.
Addressing the Rising Bharat event, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “India is now so capable that developed countries of the world are themselves coming forward to make trade deals with India.” He emphasised growing global confidence in India’s economic strength and expanding international partnerships.
Highlighting India’s growing global economic engagement, PM Modi said, “countries are coming forward to do trade deal with india,” underscoring rising international interest in partnering with India.
Prime Minister Narendra Modi asserted that “We have made india world’s growth engine.” He also said, “we have controled the inflation which was in double digit,” pointing to efforts aimed at stabilising the economy.
Speaking at the Rising Bharat event, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “In the last 11 years, energy has flowed into the consciousness of the country.” He added that “we have made our banking system strong,” highlighting structural reforms undertaken during this period.
Speaking about India’s growing global standing, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “some people are not able to believe how india managed to do trade deals with developed countries.” He explained, “why are developed countries so eager to do trade deal with india…answer is aatma vishwasi bharat,” attributing the interest to a confident and self-assured India.
Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “Even after independence, India could not come out of the mentality of slavery. We are suffering the consequences of this till date.” He further added, “unfortunately after independence india could not come out of colonial mindset, because of which we are suffering even today,” highlighting the long-term effects of that mindset.
Addressing the Rising Bharat event, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, “we want to recognise our capabilities and strength.” He noted that in the past, “we use to copy foreign countries technology,” stressing the need for greater self-reliance and confidence in India’s own potential.
“We want to recognise our capabilities and strength,” PM Modi said. “We use to copy foreign countries technology,” he added.
PM Modi said that the Summit focuses on the “internal strength of the nation,” adding that India has realised its strength in the last 11 years.
Prime Minister Narendra Modi will address the Rising Bharat Summit 2026 shortly.

“If you look up to last 12 months and if you were to pick up 3 big changes, I’d say first one is this period which reforms are happening.”
He stressed that reforms are critical for scaling up and improving the ease of doing business across sectors.
“And for scaling up, it is critical doing business. lots of sectors, if you reflect back three years, were not there. those sectors are there. And govt is putting a lot of pressure in all sectors whether it is on data centres, electronics, nuclear, medical devices. You can go on and on.”
Memani also pointed to India’s increasing global engagement through trade agreements as another major development.
“Second is the way India is now reengaging globally through FTA, bilateral agreements, I think that’s fantastic.”
“Instead of just using AI, we should work on developing them”

title: Congress's Bihar Election Funds May Have Been Used To Back AI Summit Protest, Probe Suggests
content:
Congress’s election funds may have been used to finance a protest by members of the Indian Youth Congress (IYC) at the AI Summit at Bharat Mandapam, investigators are probing.
The funds were allegedly collected for the Bihar elections, officers privy to the investigation told News18.
A Special Investigation Team (SIT) set up to probe the “organised" protest has scanned multiple bank accounts linked to those arrested in the case.
Accounts associated with the Indian Youth Congress have also been examined.
“Some amount was received in bank accounts linked to Shri Krishna Hari, the national secretary of the IYC. Questioning has indicated that the money may have been unused funds from the Bihar elections. We are examining bank accounts and the possible money trail further to establish this," one of the investigators said, requesting anonymity.
Police have found that nearly Rs 35,000 was allegedly used to arrange logistics such as printing T-shirts and placards for the protest.
Investigators said umbrellas were also printed but could not be used for unspecified reasons.
So far, at least 11 persons associated with the Indian Youth Congress have been arrested. Three of them were brought to Delhi in the early hours of Friday from Himachal Pradesh on transit remand.
Among those arrested, IYC president Uday Bhanu Chib and National Secretary Shri Krishna Hari are suspected to be the masterminds behind the protest.
Officers familiar with the probe said Hari’s bank account is linked to the IYC’s account and has received funds on multiple occasions.
“We are examining how and when the money was spent and for what purposes," another officer said, also requesting anonymity.
The February 20 protest was staged at Bharat Mandapam on the final day of the AI Summit, in the lobby area of Hall Number Five.
Four persons, National Secretary Krishna Hari, who hails from Bihar; Bihar state secretary Kundan Yadav; Eastern Uttar Pradesh vice president Ajai Kumar; and Narsimha Yadav from Telangana, were initially arrested at the spot, while a search was launched for others involved.
On Tuesday, the investigation into the protest was handed over to the Delhi Police Crime Branch.
The same day, Special Commissioner of Police (Crime Branch) Devesh Srivastava addressed the media and said the protesters attempted to breach the security cordon in front of visitors at the AI Summit, during which some police personnel were injured.
He added that the involvement of several individuals had been confirmed at the preliminary stage of the probe.
“CCTV footage was examined, and some individuals who had provided assistance were identified. Office bearers of the Indian Youth Congress are also involved in the case," the officer said.
Following the February 20 incident, Delhi Police registered a case under various sections of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), including Sections 61(2) (criminal conspiracy), 121(1) (voluntarily causing hurt or grievous hurt to deter a public servant from duty), 132 (assault or criminal force to deter a public servant from discharge of duty), 195(1) (assaulting or obstructing a public servant while suppressing a riot), 221 (obstructing a public servant in discharge of public functions), 223(A) (disobedience to an order duly promulgated by a public servant), 190 (unlawful assembly), 196 (promoting enmity between groups), 197 (acts prejudicial to national integration), and 3(5) (common intention).

title: Fewer Trackside Signals, Kavach: How Indian Railways Is Clearing The Tracks For 160 Kmph Travel
content:
The “superfast" mission looks a step closer for Indian Railways, which has undertaken a modernisation initiative to facilitate high-speed travel up to 160 kmph.
This will be reflected in how the railways manages its traffic: a move towards fewer trackside signals and a significant increase in the distance between them, as well as thorough Kavach implementation.
According to a report published by the Times of India, to ensure the safety of these faster journeys, the Railway Board has approved a proposal to double the distance between signals from the current 1 km to 2 km.
The report said this adjustment is a vital safety enhancement, as when the trains reach speeds of 160 kmph, the physics of braking dramatically change. This will require a longer stretch of track to decelerate and make a safe stop.
It said by spacing signals 2 km apart, the railways will provide locomotive pilots with a longer braking window and earlier warnings. Experts said this will significantly reduce the need for the abrupt, sudden braking that can compromise both safety and passenger comfort.
The Times of India reported that before this is implemented across the vast national network, it will undergo rigorous testing. A pilot project managed by Southern Railway has been given the “green light" to prepare a detailed proposal for the Railway Board.
It said if this pilot proves successful in maintaining safety while facilitating higher speeds, the system is expected to be rolled out nationwide with other zones like the North Central Railway already identified for future expansion. This initiative has been solidified following high-level consultations between the additional member (traction) and zonal principal chief electrical engineers, culminating in a pivotal meeting on January 27, where the 2-km spacing was officially suggested.
The shift away from traditional trackside (or “wayside") signals represents a major technological leap.
Historically, the railways has relied on physical signals — essentially sophisticated “traffic lights" on poles — that a locomotive pilot must visually spot. This system, however, will prove to be a hurdle as the network aims for higher speeds.
At speeds exceeding 130 kmph, the “sighting distance" — the time between a driver seeing a signal and reaching it — becomes dangerously short. This problem is worsened by external factors such as heavy rain, sharp curves, or the thick fog common in North India, which often forces drivers to slow down because they cannot see the signal clearly.
To overcome these constraints, the railways is transitioning to “cab signalling". Under this system, the signal aspect (red, yellow, or green) is directly displayed on a screen inside the locomotive cockpit.
This allows the train to maintain its top speed regardless of external visibility or weather conditions, effectively moving the “intelligence" of the railway from the ground into the train.
The primary driver of speed upgrade is the implementation of Kavach, India’s indigenous automatic train protection (ATP) system, alongside ETCS Level 2 (European Train Control System).
Kavach acts as an electronic “shield", creating a digital communication bubble around every train to prevent collisions. The system operates through a sophisticated “digital handshake" between several components.
This allows the railways to move from “fixed blocks" — where only one train can occupy a set section of track — to optimised or “moving" blocks. By knowing the exact position and braking distance of every train via radio communication, the gap between trains can be safely reduced, allowing more trains to run at higher speeds without waiting for a physical “all clear" from a distant pole.
Kavach also introduces continuous speed supervision. Unlike physical signals, which are “static" and only tell a driver to “stop" or “go", it provides a continuous speed curve.
The system calculates the train’s weight, the track’s gradient, and the distance to the next restriction to determine the exact moment braking is required. If a conflict is detected, Kavach initiates a three-stage safety protocol.
Additionally, it includes a network-wide SOS feature. If an accident occurs, a pilot can press an SOS button, sending a radio signal that automatically slows or stops every other train within a several-kilometre radius, preventing secondary collisions on adjacent tracks.
Beyond speed and safety, reducing trackside signals offers significant cost and maintenance benefits.
Physical signal posts are expensive to install and prone to failure, theft, or damage. Each signal requires extensive wiring, power, and individual maintenance.
By contrast, it is far more cost-effective to maintain a network of radio towers for Kavach and ETCS than thousands of individual signal lamps and hundreds of kilometres of copper signalling cables. This reduction in a “signal failure" is expected to significantly improve the punctuality of the network.
As of February, the rollout of Kavach 4.0 has significantly accelerated. The project is focused on two critical high-speed corridors under ‘Mission Raftar’.
According to the ministry of railways, the vendor base has expanded to over five companies in 2026, shifting the pace from experimental to mass production. With over 1,300 route kilometres already commissioned and more than 4,150 locomotives fitted with Kavach, the full activation of this system remains the final “permission slip" needed to officially raise the speed limit to 160 kmph across these vital corridors.

title: News18 Rising Bharat Summit 2026 Showcases India’s Sporting And Cultural Surge
content:
India’s emergence is no longer limited to economic graphs and policy. Today, it is seen on the podiums, red carpets, and virtual stages of the world. From unprecedented sporting achievements to globally acclaimed cinematic and musical accomplishments, Bharat is asserting its strength with confidence and clarity.
Capturing this defining moment, the News18 Rising Bharat Summit 2026, themed Strength Within, will bring together some of the most influential voices from sports and entertainment on 27-28 February at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi.
Indian sport is in one of its most dynamic phases. From the Women’s Cricket Team lifting the World Cup and the U-19 men reclaiming global dominance, to boxers securing nine gold medals at BOXAM, shooters commanding ISSF podium finishes, wrestlers sustaining Asian supremacy, and chess champions conquering international tournaments, the surge is both sustained and significant.
This is reflected in the fact that badminton icons Saina Nehwal, bronze medallist and previous World No. 1, and PV Sindhu, two-time Olympic medallist, will join Mohammed Shami, Mithali Raj, and Yuzvendra Chahal to talk about the importance of resilience, discipline, and the winning mindset required to not only compete globally but also win.
Parallel to its sporting ascent, Indian entertainment is commanding unprecedented international recognition. Homebound received a standing ovation at Cannes and reached the top 15 at the Oscars. All We Imagine As Light secured the Cannes Grand Prix. Boong won at the BAFTAs. Musical collaborations such as Dhurandhar – Part 1 are expanding India’s creative footprint beyond borders.
As India’s creative voice grows louder, the summit will feature cinema and storytelling voices, including Kangana Ranaut, Anil Kapoor, and Yami Gautam. Joining them are author Advaita Kala and critic Baradwaj Rangan, who will examine how narrative, identity, and influence are shaping India’s cultural discourse.
Giving it a modern touch, hip-hop and rap artists Reble (Daiaphi Lamare), who rose to fame through the popular track “Run Down the City – Monica" in Dhurandhar – Part 1, Yung DSA (Yash Deshmukh), and South Korean singer Aoora will bring to the fore the changing music scenario that is connecting with the youth of India.
Balancing performance with perspective, kirtan singer Radhika Das and balance artist Gautam Vaishnav will bring to the table conversations on mindfulness and self-discipline, while DJ Lil Aarohi, Nikhil Chinapa, and DJ Skipster will focus on the role of music in shaping identity and culture.
From sports to cinema to music, it is clear that the underlying factor is excellence achieved through inner conviction. As India moves from aspiration to achievement, the News18 Rising Bharat Summit 2026 will bring together performance and purpose, with talent and national pride.
27-28 February 2026 | Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi

title: Assam Cabinet Moves Gauhati HC, Seeks Fast-Track Court In Zubeen Garg Case
content:
The Assam Cabinet has decided to formally approach the Gauhati High Court seeking the constitution of a fast-track court to hear the high-profile death case of popular Assamese singer Zubeen Garg. The move follows mounting public pressure and demands from the singer’s family for a speedy trial.
Announcing the decision after a Cabinet meeting on February 26, Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma said the state government would request the Chief Justice of the Gauhati High Court to facilitate day-to-day hearings to ensure swift justice.
“The Assam Cabinet has decided to recommend or formally request the Gauhati High Court to constitute a fast-track court for the trial in the death case of popular Assamese singer Zubeen Garg. This move aims to expedite proceedings and ensure timely, day-to-day hearings for swift justice," Sarma said.
The case has drawn widespread attention across Assam, with opposition parties, civil society organisations, and prominent individuals repeatedly demanding faster judicial progress. A regional political party had also announced plans to launch an agitation over delays in the case.
However, some political voices have raised concerns over whether shifting the matter to a fast-track court could have implications for bail conditions or ongoing legal proceedings.
In a related development, the Gauhati High Court has ordered the reopening of a packaged drinking water unit linked to Siddharth Sarma, one of the prime accused in the case. Sarma, who served as Garg’s manager, faces allegations of mismanagement and alleged misappropriation of the singer’s funds.
The water plant Mahavir Aqua (also referred to as Mahabir Aqua) — located in an industrial area near Guwahati, had been sealed by the Special Investigation Team (SIT) during the probe. Investigators suspect that funds allegedly siphoned from Garg were invested in the business, citing it as part of the possible motive in the case.
A Public Interest Litigation (PIL) seeking the reopening of the plant was filed by Sarma’s associate, Chetan Dhirasia. The matter was heard on February 25, 2026.
In its order, the court observed that public welfare-oriented business operations should not remain indefinitely shut due to ongoing criminal proceedings against an individual. The bench stressed the need to balance the interests of justice with broader community and economic considerations.
Meanwhile, Siddharth Sarma, event manager Shyamkanu Mahanta, co-signer Amritprabha Mahanta, accompanist Sekhar Goswami, and other accused individuals remain in judicial custody.
Observers noted that during recent court appearances, the accused appeared composed, with no visible display of remorse.
The case continues to generate significant public and political interest as calls grow louder for a time-bound trial and accountability.

title: Rising Bharat Summit 2026: PM Modi, Union Ministers And Global Voices To Shape India’s Growth Agenda
content:
Positioned at a pivotal moment in India’s growth journey, the News18 Rising Bharat Summit 2026 will bring together top national leadership and international delegates at Bharat Mandapam on February 27–28. Anchored in the theme, “Strength Within," the summit stands as one of the country’s largest platforms where governance, economic ambition, and strategic thinking converge, reflecting India’s expanding influence in global conversations.
Against this backdrop, Hon’ble Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi will deliver the keynote address, giving direction to the summit’s central theme. His address will outline India’s long-term developmental roadmap, economic momentum, and strategic priorities in a rapidly shifting global order, while setting the tone for deliberations over the two-day gathering. Among the Union Cabinet ministers joining the dialogue is Piyush Goyal, Minister for Commerce and Industry, who is expected to steer conversations on trade expansion, manufacturing growth, and export competitiveness, alongside India’s deeper integration into global supply chains.
Technology and infrastructure discussions will be led by Ashwini Vaishnaw, Minister for Railways, Information & Broadcasting, and Electronics & IT, highlighting digital transformation and next-generation connectivity as pillars of growth. Beyond national leadership, the summit will host foreign delegates, diplomats, policy experts, and industry leaders, reinforcing its standing as a forum where domestic priorities align with global realities.
